title: Question about Level EEE
wikitext:
[]: Question about Level-EEE             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Sep 09, 2008 9:35 pm What the hell are we looking at here. It is at around 1:14:49. This is the elevator shaft to Central Dogma during the scene when Misato takes Shinji to Lilith. Can someone please explain what this is. It obviously looks biological. I think it resembles what is just under the skin on a human, the area between the skin and muscle.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Tue Sep 09, 2008 10:01 pm Perhaps a surface from the Black Moon, assuming they didn't screw with the continuity and the GeoFront is still Lilith's Black Moon.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 10, 2008 6:55 am Giant neurons. ...don't look at me.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Wed Sep 10, 2008 8:42 am They do indeed seem to be something along the lines of neurons. What exact purpose they serve is somewhat a mystery. My general, waffly, fanwanking explanation is that when the GeoFront was built, the organic structures from the Black Moon-egg hadn't quite gone entirely, so these are residual remains of the Black Moon, and the neurons serve (or served) the purpose of aiding the embryonic Lilith "sense" the world outside, in a way akin to a seed (that's a normal plant seed , not a Seed ) senses its outside temperature, moisture and all that. I'm guessing that might help Lilith judge when a correct time to begin growth would be, after being planted in the ground by accident... At the same time these neurons might help the now-crucified Lilith have some idea of what goes on in the outside world, not unlike the Eva Units, while silent, are distinctly aware of what they and the pilots are going through. This is all B latant S peculation, of course, to quote Reichu.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Sep 10, 2008 8:48 am It's probably a vagina, everything else in Eva is a vagina. Damn sexual imagery.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Wed Sep 10, 2008 8:52 am It's a womb.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Wed Sep 10, 2008 8:56 am Yeah that seems plausible as well. It was originally to house the growing embryo of a Seed of Life, right.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Sep 10, 2008 9:14 am I just noticed this, but does anyone else see the Tree of Life in the upper left corner. Or is it just me......

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Sep 10, 2008 9:28 am It looks a lot like the pathways of the cerebellum. For some reason, that jumped out at me right away when I watched Rebuild for the first time. I even mentioned it in the IRC a few times a ways back. Reichu agrees with me, right? Reichu wrote: Giant neurons. ...don't look at me.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Wed Sep 10, 2008 9:39 am So, what do you think they do? (or did?)

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Sep 10, 2008 10:26 am AyrYntake wrote: So, what do you think they do? (or did?)

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Sep 10, 2008 10:51 am Action_Bastard wrote: I just noticed this, but does anyone else see the Tree of Life in the upper left corner. Or is it just me......

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Sep 10, 2008 10:53 am Action_Bastard wrote: I just noticed this, but does anyone else see the Tree of Life in the upper left corner. Or is it just me......

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:15 pm BrikHaus wrote: Reichu agrees with me, right?

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:24 pm Action_Bastard wrote: I just noticed this, but does anyone else see the Tree of Life in the upper left corner. Or is it just me......

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:28 pm The Tree of Life? I think not, atleast not in the sense it's from The End. And besides, I'm sure a lot of people on here would love to argue about the sequel theory.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:43 pm its more or less just the inside of an Egg/womb..I say this cause I'm sure "mother" needed this while on her way to Earth. She was in an Egg but since shes also alive she would be in a womb....It's both..If you follow me. A super large flying womb Egg... I went back and re watched ROE on my big PC screen...Many things I did not notice such as 00 with most of its armor burned off looking like shes in a lot of pain and the picture stated above.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:44 pm I honestly doubt that's the Tree of Life

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:50 pm It's just cool imagery, just like the SAME ELEVATOR in the original series looked like DNA.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:53 pm Yeah, I guess so. such as 00 with most of its armor burned off looking like shes in a lot of pain
